title: verbascum armenum var. armenum
intro: Verbascum armenum var. armenum, commonly known as Armenian mullein, is a biennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Eastern Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, making it a valuable remedy for respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The leaves and flowers of the Armenian mullein plant are used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to have various health benefits, including reducing anxiety and improving sleep quality. Additionally, the plant's medicinal properties have been used to treat skin conditions like eczema and wounds, as well as to soothe sore throats and relieve coughs.
